title: Fix OAuth login leaves user in inconsistent state on failure
description:
## Bug Report

### Problem
When OAuth login fails during workspace creation (after user is saved to DB), the user remains in an inconsistent state:
- User is created in the DB but has no ExternalLogin record and no Workspace
- Next OAuth attempt triggers LinkingRequiredException (asks for password) because the system finds the user by email but no matching ExternalLogin

### Root Cause
In OAuthLoginCommandHandler.CreateNewUserAsync() (line 140), SaveChangesAsync is called prematurely — BEFORE workspace creation. If workspace creation fails after this point, the user is already committed to the database but the ExternalLogin and Workspace are not.

### Fix
Remove the premature SaveChangesAsync calls from CreateNewUserAsync and CreateDefaultWorkspaceAsync. Let the single SaveChangesAsync at the end of Handle() persist everything atomically.

### Additional Issue
WorkspaceSlug.Create requires a minimum of 3 characters, but GenerateSlug can produce slugs shorter than 3 chars (e.g., email prefix 'ab'). This can trigger a ValidationException during workspace creation for users with short email prefixes.

### Acceptance Criteria
1. All entities (User, ExternalLogin, Workspace, WorkspaceMember) are saved atomically in a single SaveChangesAsync call
2. If any part of the OAuth login creation fails, nothing is persisted
3. Short email prefixes (<3 chars) generate valid workspace slugs
4. Second OAuth attempt succeeds if the first attempt failed
close_reason: Fixed: removed premature SaveChangesAsync from CreateNewUserAsync and CreateDefaultWorkspaceAsync. All entities now persist atomically via single SaveChangesAsync at end of Handle(). Also fixed slug min-length issue.

title: [plugin:vite:import-analysis] Failed to resolve import ../../data/mockAI from src/components/repurpose/ConfigBar.tsx
description: ConfigBar.tsx imports \mockGenerateRepurpose\ from \../../data/mockAI\ but the file does not exist. This causes a Vite build failure that blocks the dev server. The import at line 6 of D:/Code/Syncra/fe/src/components/repurpose/ConfigBar.tsx needs to be fixed by either creating the missing mockAI file or removing/updating the import.
close_reason: Created missing mockAI.ts with mockGenerateRepurpose function and RepurposePlatform re-export

title: Fix Zernio post creation platform validation error
description: Set platform specific data instead of null when creating posts via Zernio API to prevent deserialization schema mismatch errors.

title: Fix platform panel display logic
description: Update useCreatePostState activePlatforms default values to ensure platform sections are only shown when selected.

title: Fix platform selection, default preview platform, and improve account selection UI
description: Fix issue where TikTok is previewed by default when only Facebook Pages are connected. Correctly derive active platforms and active tab based on selected social accounts, and redesign account selection UI to support multiple accounts clearly.

title: Fix too long avatar URL for social accounts select-page
description: The Facebook select-page endpoint fails to save social accounts if the avatar URL exceeds 500 characters. We need to increase AvatarUrlMaxLength in SocialAccount, update the EF Core configuration, and apply a database migration.
close_reason: Fixed too long avatar URL for social accounts select-page by increasing the max length of AvatarUrl/ParticipantAvatarUrl in SocialAccount, ZernioProfile, InboxConversation, and UserProfile to 2048, and applying EF migrations.

title: Fix UI reload on batch post deletion failure
description: When deleting posts, if some posts fail to delete (e.g. published posts return bad request), Promise.all rejects, preventing the UI from reloading the successfully deleted posts. Use Promise.allSettled to handle partial successes.

title: Redesign Create Post modal and implement new features
description: Redesign the Create Post modal to support multi-workspace posting, platform-specific customization, platform grouping, and timezone overrides.

title: Fix Delete button in frontend to use Zernio Delete API when applicable
description: The Delete button in the frontend (PostsOverviewPage) always calls the general delete API which does not cancel scheduled posts in Zernio. It should check if the post has a zernioPostId and call the deleteZernioPost API if present.

title: Implement bulk post deletion and hover checkbox selection
Add Posts Sidebar Section and Overview Page
description: Implement a Posts section in the sidebar with Overview and Queues sub-menus, remove the sidebar New Post button, and implement the Posts Overview page with grid, table, and calendar views using Zapier design guidelines.

title: Add loading spinner and state to disconnect button
description: Show a rotating spinner and 'Disconnecting...' text on the disconnect button of the card currently being disconnected.

title: Add custom confirmation modal for disconnecting account
description: Replace browser confirm window with a styled confirmation modal matching DESIGN.md guidelines when disconnecting a social account connection.

title: Redesign Create Post modal to Zernio two-column layout
description: Sửa bố cục giao diện UI UX của Create Post modal thành bố cục zernio: có first comment, các option feed/story/reel tùy theo nền tảng, và các publishing option: schedule, now, queue, save draft.

title: 26-02: Zernio post lifecycle webhook handlers and tests
description: Implement post.scheduled, post.published, post.failed, post.partial, and post.cancelled handlers in ProcessZernioWebhookJob with cross-workspace and terminal-state guards, and write unit tests.

title: 26-02: IPostStatusNotifier abstraction and implementation
description: Create IPostStatusNotifier interface in Application, concrete implementation in Infrastructure/Api, and register in DI to push SignalR updates from Hangfire webhook job.

title: 20-04: Frontend forgot/reset pages + LoginModal link
description: Create ForgotPasswordPage, ResetPasswordPage, CSS modules, API client methods, routes in App.tsx, and Forgot password link in LoginModal. Wave 3 — depends on 20-02 and 20-03.

title: 20-03: ResetPassword command + handler + endpoint
description: Create ResetPasswordCommand + handler with token validation, BCrypt hashing, user session invalidation, and POST /auth/reset-password endpoint. Wave 2 — depends on 20-01.

title: 20-02: Postmark email service + ForgotPassword command + endpoint
description: Create PostmarkOptions, IEmailService interface, PostmarkEmailService (HttpClient-based), DI registration, ForgotPasswordCommand + handler with rate limiting, and POST /auth/forgot-password endpoint. Wave 2 — depends on 20-01.

title: 20-01: PasswordResetToken entity + repository + EF migration
description: Create PasswordResetToken entity, IPasswordResetTokenRepository interface, repository implementation, AppDbContext update, DI registration, and EF migration. Wave 1 — no dependencies.

title: Implement Google OAuth authentication
description:
Add Google Sign-In as an authentication option alongside existing email/password auth.

**Backend:**
- Add Google OAuth provider to ASP.NET Core authentication
- Create endpoint for Google OAuth callback
- Handle token exchange and user creation/linking
- Issue JWT token after successful Google auth

**Frontend:**
- Add Google Sign-In button to login modal
- Handle Google OAuth redirect flow
- Integrate with existing auth context

**Security:**
- Validate Google ID tokens server-side
- Handle account linking (existing email = Google email)
close_reason: v1.5 milestone complete - all 5 phases (15-19) finished, 13/13 plans done

title: Merge improve-be into main and clean up branch
description: Merge improve-be branch into main to bring all Phase 1-14 work to main branch, then delete improve-be branch. Steps: 1) Commit uncommitted changes on improve-be, 2) Push improve-be, 3) Checkout main, 4) Merge improve-be into main, 5) Push main to remote, 6) Delete improve-be locally and remotely, 7) Verify.
